Import necessary modules including apex, argparse, csv, itertools, logging, modeling, schedulers, time, tokenization_utils, torch, tqdm, and utils
Define hyperparameters such as batch size, learning rate, gradient accumulation steps, etc.
Load pre-trained student model and tokenizer using from_pretrained function from modeling module
Load pre-trained teacher model if available for distillation or initialize a new one if scratch training is required
Create synthetic input data tensors with random values to mimic real-world inputs
Define the project head for dimensionality reduction or feature extraction if it exists in your setup
Initialize an optimizer (e.g., FusedAdam) and learning rate scheduler
Set up a logging configuration using basicConfig from logging module to log intermediate steps
Initialize a training loop with specified number of epochs
During each epoch, perform forward pass through student model, calculate loss using student-teacher discrepancy or some other metric
Perform backward pass and accumulate gradients over specified gradient accumulation steps
Clip gradients if necessary to prevent exploding gradients
Perform optimizer step after accumulating gradients for the specified number of steps
Log training metrics such as loss at each step
After completing one epoch, evaluate model performance on a validation set (if available)
Assert that during the forward pass through student-teacher models or within the loss calculation, NaN values are encountered in loss tensor
Monitor memory usage using appropriate functions like torch.cuda.memory_allocated() to verify if GPU memory usage exceeds expected threshold
